behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because the failure is at a module boundary: PR-changed-file discovery must be handed to the analysis runner correctly. Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit is too low if it only tests a filter/helper; it can miss wiring bugs where unchanged files are still dispatched. Upgrade check: System rejected because behavior text does not require real GitHub network/OS/subprocess timing, only correct changed-file scope. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no AI/ML dependency; no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire `changed_files_provider` + `analysis_dispatcher` + scanner selection in-process with a fake PR diff containing changed and unchanged paths. Assert only changed paths are sent to analysis and unchanged paths are never scanned.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because findings aggregation and comment formatting/post payload creation are multiple modules interacting. Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit could validate pure grouping logic but may miss contract mismatches between aggregator and comment renderer. Upgrade check: System rejected because behavior text focuses on grouping structure in the posted comment content, not real GitHub API transport behavior. Integration-brake: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess requirement; no AI/ML dependency; no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run analyzer-output adapter + severity grouper + comment builder together using deterministic findings fixture and a fake PR-comment client. Assert rendered comment has `error`, `warning`, and `info` sections with correct membership/counts.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: System, because the failure boundary is explicit GitHub Actions timeout/runtime behavior. REAL-INFRA applies: wall-clock completion under CI runtime constraints is infrastructure-dependent. Pass 2 downgrade check: Integration load test with mocks can underestimate startup/I/O/process overhead and hide timeout failures. Upgrade check: Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI/ML output quality or multi-agent chain is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the lint step as the real CI job entrypoint (same command/env as GitHub Action) against a synthetic 200-file PR fixture and measure end-to-end wall time. Fail if runtime exceeds a conservative threshold below 10 minutes (e.g., 9m30s) to preserve Actions margin.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: System, because behavior explicitly involves a crashing linter process (real subprocess failure mode) and continued orchestration afterward. MOCK-HIDE: exception stubs may miss real crash semantics/exit handling. Pass 2 downgrade check: Integration with mocked crash can pass while real subprocess termination handling fails. Upgrade check: Agentic/Workflow rejected; no AI/ML dependency or multi-agent sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with the real linter subprocess on a corpus including one malformed file that triggers an actual linter crash/abnormal exit. Assert pipeline continues analyzing other files and still emits a PR comment payload with surviving findings plus crash notice.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit, because this is primarily deterministic dedup logic on findings data (same file/line/rule overlap criteria). Pass 2 downgrade check: no lower level than Unit. Upgrade check: Integration rejected because behavior text does not require module-boundary failures; it can be fully exercised in one dedup function/object with crafted inputs. System/Agentic/Workflow disqualified by lack of infrastructure and AI requirements.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup function/object with a fixture containing overlapping-rule findings targeting the same file/line and distinct findings on other lines. Assert output keeps one canonical entry per duplicate set and preserves non-duplicates.
